vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

requirements: Carry wing spar loads into fuselage spine
Allow camera operator to view and adjust camera settings

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues in the avionics bay due to insufficient cooling.
risk.mit: Implement additional cooling solutions such as heat sinks or active cooling systems.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure if the wing spar loads exceed the designed capacity.

risk.mit: Review and optimize the design for improved accessibility.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Wing spar carrying nacelle loads into fuselage spine; V-tail/ruddervator and landing gear attach structure

physics_paths.path_description: Loads from nacelle tilt mechanism are transferred through wing spar to fuselage spine.
Service ports provide access for maintenance and replacement of motor servos.
Heat from sun exposure is dissipated through cooling fins on the wing surface.

internal_feature_targets.description: Dual 15mm CFRP spar running along the wing's centerline.
Socket for connecting the wing spar to the fuselage spine.
Attachment point for the fuselage spine.
Mounting point for the V-tail structure.
Attachment point for the ruddervator control surfaces.
Stabilizer mount for additional structural support.
Mounting point for the landing gear assembly.
Support leg for the landing gear.
Access cut for servicing and maintenance.

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.support_orientation: Support structures oriented for optimal FDM print direction
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit, CMM for dimensional accuracy

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

physics_paths.description: Loads from wing structure transferred through bearing seats to tilt mechanism
Torque from servo module drives worm gear for nacelle tilt
Heat generated by motor dissipates through heat sink fins
Rotation around tilt axis enables nacelle tilt from 0 to 90 degrees
Access for motor and ESC replacement

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Install band clamps after wing attachment

integration_opportunities.description: Optimize tilt axis for higher torque handling
Increase surface area of heat sink fins for better cooling
Design access panels for easier motor and ESC replacement

manufacturing_strategy.orientation: Build up from wing spar socket

verification_focus.description: Verify bearing seat can handle expected loads from wing structure
Confirm nacelle can tilt from 0 to 90 degrees without obstruction
Ensure motor and ESC can be easily replaced through access panel
